mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate from elevated levels signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations. Lower FEDFUNDS directly compresses required returns, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as investors rebalance into equities.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.33% as of May 2009 reflects recent Fed rate cuts from crisis highs
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS declines by > 0.25pp month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A stabilization or decline in the unemployment rate signals labor market recovery and rising consumer confidence. When UNRATE falls below a critical threshold after a spike, equity investors interpret this as confirmation of economic recovery, driving positive forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations improve.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 4.0% after rising above 5.0% in prior 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7% in May 2009 reflects recent labor market stabilization from crisis peak
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or correlation between monthly UNRATE changes and 60-day forward SPX returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A joint expansion in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals broad-based consumer demand recovery. When both series rise simultaneously above their 6-month moving averages, this interaction confirms demand-side momentum and predicts positive equity returns over 20 trading days as earnings revisions accelerate.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their respective 6-month moving averages in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 705,692M and HOUST at 1,372K units in April 2009 show early recovery signals
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 52% of co-triggered instances despite both RSAFS and HOUST rising.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above its 3-month trend, signaling supply-side capacity utilization recovery. This mechanism predicts positive 5-day returns as investors interpret rising production as confirmation that the recession trough has passed and corporate margins will expand.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5% after 3 consecutive months of decline or stagnation
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 102.67 in May 2009 reflects early stabilization from crisis lows
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances, or the predictive power of INDPRO acceleration decays to Rank IC < 0.02 in any rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) contraction decelerates—the month-over-month decline shrinks—signaling that labor market deterioration is slowing. This deceleration of job losses, even before employment turns positive, triggers forward-looking equity gains over 20 trading days as investors anticipate the inflection point.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month decline shrinks to less than 0.3% after prior month decline exceeded 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 157,087K in May 2009 reflects ongoing but decelerating job losses from 2008 crisis
regime_hint: labor market inflection from contraction to stabilization
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 54% of triggered instances, or Spearman rank correlation between PAYEMS deceleration and 20-day forward returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 1.0% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows month-over-month stability or deflation, real rates turn deeply negative. This real rate compression incentivizes equity allocation over cash, producing positive 60-day returns as portfolio rebalancing accelerates.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 1.0% AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month change is between -0.5% and +0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.33% and CPIAUCSL at 307.92 in May 2009 reflect post-crisis monetary accommodation
regime_hint: zero-bound monetary policy with disinflation pressure
failure_condition: The interaction term (FEDFUNDS < 1.0% AND low CPIAUCSL growth) produces Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 51% of co-triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter turns positive after two consecutive quarters of contraction, signaling recession end. This inflection point triggers a 60-day equity rally as investors shift from recession hedges to cyclical positioning and earnings expectations reset upward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth turns positive after two consecutive quarters of negative growth
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 22,506.4B (2017 dollars) as of Q1 2009 reflects ongoing recession with inflection expected in Q2-Q3 2009
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of instances following positive GDPC1 inflection, or the lead-lag relationship between GDPC1 inflection and SPX returns shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) both stabilize simultaneously—INDPRO stops declining and PAYEMS deceleration slows—signaling synchronized supply and labor recovery. This dual stabilization predicts positive 20-day returns as investors gain confidence in broad-based economic recovery.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change turns positive AND PAYEMS month-over-month decline shrinks to < 0.2% in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 102.67 and PAYEMS at 157,087K in May 2009 show early stabilization signals
regime_hint: synchronized supply and labor stabilization in post-crisis recovery
failure_condition: The joint stabilization signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 53% of co-triggered instances despite both INDPRO and PAYEMS stabilizing.
